seoTitle: How to Generate PDF Files From Airtable Data
metaDescription: Design custom PDF templates in Airtable's Page Designer extension and export your database records as professional documents.
intro: Airtable's Page Designer extension lets you build a custom document template — invoices, reports, or anything else — and populate it directly from your table records rather than manually copying data into another program. This guide covers installing the extension, laying out your template, and exporting the finished documents as PDFs.

steps.heading: Open the Extensions directory
steps.body: Navigate to the Extensions menu in your Airtable base to find available add-ons.

steps.heading: Install Page Designer
steps.body: Install the specialized Page Designer block from the extensions list.

steps.heading: Configure your page layout
steps.body: Set custom page dimensions, margins, and typography to establish the template canvas for your document.

steps.heading: Map your fields to the layout
steps.body: Drag and drop dynamic text and image elements from your table directly onto the document canvas.

steps.heading: Preview across records
steps.body: Toggle through individual records to check that live data integrates correctly across your customized layout.

steps.heading: Export as PDF
steps.body: Use the system print dialog through the extension to batch export and download your formatted records as PDFs.

faq.question: Do I need to design a new template for every record?
faq.answer: No, you design the template once and Page Designer populates it dynamically as you toggle between individual records.

faq.question: Can I include images from my Airtable attachments in the PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag image fields from your table directly into the document layout alongside text fields.

faq.question: Is Page Designer included free with Airtable?
faq.answer: Page Designer is available as an installable extension, though access can depend on your Airtable plan tier.

faq.question: Can I export multiple records as PDFs at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the extension supports batch exporting so you can generate PDFs for several records in one pass rather than one at a time.
